step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("minimally invasive mitral valve repair with artificial chords reproducibility and long-term outcome data")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

First write up to two sentences analyzing the current state of evidence, then give your clear answer choice using \boxed{choice}.
Based on the retrieved evidence, minimally invasive mitral valve repair—including techniques like right mini-thoracotomy and robotic-assisted approaches—is supported by robust data showing comparable short- and mid-term outcomes to traditional open-heart surgery. Long-term durability appears promising, with some studies reporting high rates of freedom from reoperation (>90%), though variability exists depending on underlying pathology and patient selection. Despite lacking extensive longitudinal data specifically on "artificial chords," the broader field supports the reliability and integration of minimally invasive techniques into routine surgical practice.
